VERDICT: TAMPERED
ROUND: 20
REASONING: In round 20 the value called “step1” should be the modular sum of h and S1.  
• h (line 349) = 1110 1101 0101 0101 1001 1000 0010 1011 (hex ED55982B)  
• S1 (line 350) = 1100 0011 0110 1001 0101 0110 1100 1001 (hex C36956C9)

Adding them modulo 2³² gives  
ED55982B + C36956C9 = B0BEEFF4 (binary 1011 0000 1011 1110 1110 1111 1111 0100)

But the trace records for step1 (line 352) the unchanged value  
1110 1101 0101 0101 1001 1000 0010 1011 (hex ED55982B), i.e. h itself.  
The subsequent value step2 (line 353) is consistent with this wrong step1 (it equals h + ch), so every later quantity is built on this erroneous word.  
Because the first discrepancy appears in round 20 and differs by many bits, the trace must have been altered there; it is not the authentic SHA-256 computation.
